data.title: Larry Fink Says Oil Could Crash Below $50 If War Leaves Iran 'Neutralized': 'Great Probability'
data.description:
BlackRock Inc. (NYSE:BLK) CEO Larry Fink expressed his belief that the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict will not have lasting economic consequences, even as oil prices continue to rise.

During his appearance on Fox News’ “Special Report” on Wednesday, Fink dismissed the notion of a prolonged war. He also predicted a potential drop in oil prices, possibly even below pre-war levels, once the conflict ends and if “neutralized” Iran re-enters the global market.

“I mean, there’s probably a great probability that oil is gonna be below 50,” said Fink.

The CEO of the world’s largest asset manager downplayed the short-term impacts on energy prices, stating that the majority of the $14.5 trillion managed by BlackRock is long-dated, hence the short-term market volatility doesn’t concern him much.

Despite the recent surge in oil prices, Fink advised investors against making hasty decisions. He expressed concern over many people withdrawing from the market, terming it “the wrong outcome.”

 “Buy more here….This is a good long-term opportunity,” said Fink.

Oil Surge Risk Amid Hormuz Tensions

Earlier this month, Fundstrat’s Tom Lee described the recent volatility as a typical “risk-premium expansion,” not a structural breakdown. He expects markets to rebound into late March and potentially strengthen through April.

At the same time, JP Morgan cautioned investors that the TACO trade may not be the best strategy during this phase. Instead, he suggested considering alternative investments, particularly infrastructure assets.

Meanwhile, Iranian military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaqari warned Washington on Wednesday that oil could go beyond $200 per barrel, accusing it of destabilizing the regional security. The warning comes as tensions threaten shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, which carries about one-fifth of global oil supplies.

When last checked, WTI crude was trading over 4% higher at $91.09 per barrel.

Disclaimer: This content was partially produced with the help of AI tools and was reviewed and published by a Benzinga editor.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Millennials Could Control $140 Trillion by 2045 — The Wealth Strategy Behind the Surge
data.description:
Quick Summary

  • New projections suggest millennials' net worth could grow more than 11% annually and reach about $140 trillion by 2045, driven by higher savings rates and long‑term bets on equities, pensions, real estate, and other growth assets.
  • If you want to build your own version of that playbook, you can answer a short SmartAsset questionnaire and get matched with up to three vetted fiduciary advisors who can tailor decisions to your actual numbers.

Millennials, once cast as the generation that might never catch up, are seeing projections that suggest the old narrative is badly out of date.

A commissioned analysis by Oxford Economics finds that millennial households' net wealth is expected to grow by more than 11% a year over the next two decades—nearly double the 5.8% projected for the overall U.S. population. 

By 2045, that puts millennials on track to control roughly $140 trillion in net wealth, and that figure doesn't even count future inheritances. This is a generation that, despite the headlines, is building its own wealth through saving and investing, not just waiting for a great wealth transfer.

The assets doing the heavy lifting

One reason the forecast is so aggressive is where millennials keep their money. According to the Oxford Economics report, most of their assets are concentrated in four big buckets which good planners obsess over. 

That includes real estate, equities and other investments, pensions (including 401(k)s), and liquid assets like cash.

Those categories aren't just large, they're projected to grow quickly. Over the next 20 years, millennial real estate holdings are expected to grow about 7% annually, equities by nearly 13%, and pensions by roughly 13.6% a year. 

By 2045, millennials are projected to hold around $30 trillion in pensions, $28 trillion in other assets (including cash), $22 trillion in equities, and $21 trillion in real estate.

Sticking with the plan

The Oxford Economics report also finds millennials are saving at a higher clip than previous generations at the same age. Over the past decade, their savings rate has exceeded the national average by about 4.9%. 

Baby boomers also out‑saved the national average at that stage of life, but only by about 2.5%. Looking ahead, millennials' savings rate is projected to pull even further ahead, with the gap vs. the national average peaking around 14% as they approach 50.

That's happening without wildly different balance sheets. Millennial debt levels look broadly similar to those of earlier generations at comparable ages. The difference is where the extra dollars go. Instead of waiting to be debt‑free first, more of this cohort is pushing money into retirement accounts, equities, and real estate while time is still on their side.

The hard part is translating that into your own life. How much should you actually be saving? How aggressive can you be with equities? When does it make sense to prioritize a 401(k), a Roth IRA, or a down payment—and what gets cut to make room? 

Those aren't questions a generic rule of thumb can really solve.

That's where professional guidance comes in. If you want to see where you're at, answer this short questionnaire and SmartAsset's free matching tool pairs you with up to three advisors who actively work with clients like you. 

From there, you can compare approaches and use those conversations to turn the broad millennial playbook into a plan that actually fits your numbers.

Image: Imagn

data.title: What's Going On With IBM Stock On Thursday?
data.description:
International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) on Thursday unveiled a quantum-centric supercomputing architecture that integrates quantum and classical computing to tackle complex scientific problems.

The system is designed to evolve over time, combining quantum hardware with classical infrastructure to support demanding workloads in fields such as chemistry and materials science. According to IBM Research Director Jay Gambetta, the approach allows quantum processors to handle the most complex parts of scientific problems while working alongside traditional high-performance computing systems.

The architecture also includes orchestration tools and open software frameworks so developers can access quantum capabilities using familiar platforms, positioning IBM to advance scientific discovery and computational efficiency.

The announcement comes as broader markets declined in the previous session, with the Nasdaq Composite falling 0.32% and the S&P 500 down 0.37%.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 1.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 15.2% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend in the short to medium term.

Shares have decreased 0.30% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 42.00, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD shows a value of -8.6921, with the signal line at -11.1704, indicating bullish momentum as the MACD is above the signal line. The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum for the stock.

  • Key Resistance: $296.00
  • Key Support: $220.50

Incorporated in 1911, International Business Machines, or IBM, is one of the oldest technology companies in the world. It provides software, IT consulting services, and hardware to help business customers modernize their technology workflows. IBM operates in 175 countries and employs approximately 300,000 people.

The company has a robust roster of business partners to service its clients, which includes 95% of all Fortune 500 companies. IBM’s products, including Red Hat, watsonx, and mainframes, handle some of the world’s most important data workloads in areas like finance and retail.

The recent announcement about quantum-centric supercomputing is significant as it showcases IBM’s commitment to innovation in the tech space. This development could enhance its competitive edge in the rapidly evolving landscape of quantum computing and high-performance computing.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

International Business Machines Corporation is slated to provide its next financial update on April 22, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: $1.80 (Up from $1.60)
  • Revenue Estimate: $15.60 Billion (Up from $14.54 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 22.3x (Indicates fair valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $318.92. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Wedbush: Outperform (Maintains Target to $340.00) (Feb. 25)
  • Morgan Stanley: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $247.00) (Feb. 25)
  • UBS: Upgraded to Neutral (Maintains Target to $236.00) (Feb. 25)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for International Business Machines, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 25.01 — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality: 80.54 — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum: 27.92 — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: International Business Machines’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the Quality score indicates a solid balance sheet, the low Value and Momentum scores suggest that the stock may be facing challenges in the current market environment.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because IBM carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

IBM Stock Price Activity: IBM shares were down 0.78% at $246.93 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Nvidia-Palantir Partnership Targets Data-Sovereign AI Deployment
data.description:
NVIDIA Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) shares slid during Thursday's premarket session. Today, the company announced partnership news tied to government-grade AI deployments while risk appetite stayed cautious.

Partnership With Palantir

Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) announced a "sovereign AI OS reference architecture" with Nvidia.

The company says it delivers customers a turnkey AI datacenter experience spanning hardware procurement through application deployment.

Palantir said the AIOS-RA is based on NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures. It is "tested and qualified" to run Palantir's full software suite, including AIP, Foundry, Apollo, Rubix, and AIP Hub.

The release frames the stack as combining NVIDIA AI infrastructure and notes it runs on NVIDIA Blackwell.

Justin Boitano, vice president, Enterprise AI Platforms, NVIDIA, added, "AI is redefining the infrastructure stack — demanding, latency-sensitive and data-sovereign environments require a full-stack architecture — built from silicon to systems to software."

"By combining Palantir's sovereign AI OS reference architecture with NVIDIA AI infrastructure, industries and nations can turn data into intelligence with speed, efficiency, and trust."

Technical Analysis

The broader market moved lower on the previous trading day, with the S&P 500 (SPY) down 0.49% and the Nasdaq (QQQ) down 0.44%.

NVIDIA is trading 0.3% below its 20-day SMA and 0.4% below its 100-day SMA, showing the stock is chopping around a tight consolidation zone after a strong run.

Shares are up 60.73% over the past 12 months, and the stock is positioned closer to its 52-week high ($212.19) than its 52-week low ($86.62).

Analyst Outlook

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $277.50. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Tigress Financial: Strong Buy (Raises Target to $360.00) (Mar. 5)
  • Wedbush: Outperform (Raises Target to $300.00) (Mar. 3)
  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Raises Target to $265.00) (Feb. 26)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Nvidia, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: Nvidia's Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic "high-growth leader" profile, with Momentum and Quality both firmly bullish. The weak Value score is the main trade-off—investors are paying up for growth, so technical support levels tend to matter more when sentiment turns risk-off.

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because NVDA carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

NVDA Price Action: Nvidia shares were down 0.76% at $184.62 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Pacira Faces Board Fight As Activist Investor Criticizes CEO Pay, Missed Earnings
data.description:
DOMA Perpetual Capital Management on Wednesday nominated three candidates to Pacira BioSciences Inc.s (NASDAQ:PCRX) board, citing concerns over the company’s performance and management oversight, adding pressure as broader markets edged lower.

Inadequate Financial Controls at Pacira BioSciences

DOMA Perpetual, which holds approximately 7.1% of Pacira’s outstanding shares, has expressed that the current board has failed to provide adequate financial controls and oversight, contributing to a significant decline in the stock price over the years.

The firm highlighted that under CEO Frank Lee, the stock has fallen 30%, and it has consistently missed earnings expectations while management compensation has soared.

Need To Change Leadership

In its statement, DOMA emphasized the need for a change in leadership to restore shareholder confidence and maximize returns.

“Management executive compensation is unsustainable, currently approaching 7% of the Firm’s entire market capitalization,” DOMA said in a press release on Wednesday.

The firm believes that the board should engage bankers to explore a sale of the company and discontinue future acquisitions, arguing that the current management has proven incapable of meeting performance-based goals.

What Pacira Has To Say

Pacira BioSciences Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 1.59% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 2.63% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term strength.

Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 3.58% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 53.42, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is at 0.1205, above its signal line at 0.1046, indicating bullish momentum.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $23.00
  • Key Support: $22.50

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $34.75. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Maintains Target to $38.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $30.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Barclays: Initiated with Equal-Weight (Target $27.00) (Dec. 9, 2025)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Pacira BioSciences, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 59.78 — The stock is trading at a moderate valuation compared to peers.
  • Quality: 36.88 — Indicates some concerns regarding the company’s operational effectiveness.
  • Momentum: 22.85 — Suggests the stock is underperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Pacira BioSciences’ Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the Value score indicates some potential for recovery, the low Momentum score suggests that the stock is currently struggling to gain traction in the market.

PCRX Stock Price Activity: Pacira BioSciences shares were up 0.18% at $22.87 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
